segments.title: 夜行儿童 / Night Walking Kids
segments.summary: 讲述了一个小镇发生的神秘事件：17名学生在同一时间凌晨2:17集体离家出走，消失在黑暗中。唯一留下的学生Alex和老师Justine Gandy成为怀疑对象。警方调查无果，家长们情绪激动，指责老师知情却隐瞒。学校被迫重新开放，笼罩在一片恐慌与猜疑之中。

segments.title: 界限的试探 / Testing Boundaries
segments.summary: 教师Justine因过度亲近学生被警告，禁止她与受伤学生Alex接触。与此同时，她的车被人写上'女巫'标签，暗示社区对她的排斥。与旧情人Paul的重逢引发复杂情感纠葛，而她介入调查失踪儿童案件也受到质疑，职业与个人界限在多重压力下变得模糊。

segments.title: 质疑与寻找 / Questioning & Searching
segments.summary: 警方与焦急的家长讨论孩子失踪案，家长怀疑某女教师隐瞒真相并要求调查其背景。主角同时不断寻找一个叫Matthew的孩子，最终表达歉意和深切的情感，引发对真相的进一步质疑。

segments.title: 警告与宽恕 / Warning & Forgiveness
segments.summary: Matthew父亲Archer与另一失踪儿童家人交换线索却引发争执。警察暴力制服嫌疑人James后私下释放并警告他。社区笼罩在17名儿童失踪的恐慌中，邻里关系紧张，彼此猜疑。

segments.title: 困境与线索 / Predicament & Clue
segments.summary: Paul因团队任务外出回家，情绪激动地寻求经济来源以支付新工作需要的服装。他尝试与母亲沟通、售卖物品，却屡屡碰壁。在愤怒与绝望中，他意外发现了失踪儿童线索，并拨打举报电话寻求50000美元奖励。

segments.title: 致命陷阱 / Deadly Trap
segments.summary: 自称Alex姑妈的Gladys到访学校，拒绝让校长与孩子父母交谈。她随后到访校长家中，表面友好却突然行凶，刺伤校长并威胁其不得报警。这个神秘女子看似帮助寻找被绑架儿童，实则隐藏危险意图。

segments.title: 武器化的人 / The Weaponized
segments.summary: 商店内发生暴力事件后，两人意识到Matthew和另一女孩的行为相似，暗示他们被某种力量操控。同时，家庭成员接待身体状况不佳的亲戚Gladys，而当她出现后，父母行为异常，似乎受到她的控制。家庭氛围逐渐紧张，暗示更深层次的秘密正在浮现。

segments.title: 被迫的沉默 / Forced Silence
segments.summary: 神秘威胁者强迫Alex承诺不泄露其父母状况及自己的存在，并展示可怕能力以证明若违约将导致父母互相伤害。随后，威胁者要求Alex从学校带回每位同学的物品，声称这可能治好自己的病。Alex在极度恐惧中保持沉默，承受着巨大心理压力。

segments.title: 逃离与庇护 / Escape & Shelter
segments.summary: Alex一家面临威胁，警察介入后出现紧张对峙。Paul情绪失控，家庭陷入危机。最终Alex与父母分离，被送往新的安全住所，远离潜在危险环境，获得庇护和新的开始。

subtitles.text: It happened right here in my town two years ago.
A lot of people die in a lot of really weird ways
but you're not gonna find it in the news
because the police and the top people in this town
that they weren't able to solve it
that they covered everything all up.
But if you come here and ask anyone,
they'll all tell you the same thing
that I'm gonna tell you now.
This story starts in my school.
is like a normal day for the whole school.
And they had this teacher who was new.
And on this day, she was going to her classroom
Today, none of her kids were there.
Every other class at that school had all their kids.
Even the other third grade Mrs. Belt taught was full.
But Mrs. Gandy's room was totally empty.
And his name was Alex Lilly.
And he was the only kid in the class of 18
that came to school that day.
He was the only one there
because the night before at 2:17 in the morning,
walked across the front yard and into the dark.

The police could tell that the kids left at 2:17
because, like, half the houses had alarms
that got tripped when they walked outside.
Some of the kids even got videoed
by the houses that had cameras.
But the cameras only showed the kids
They didn't show where they went after that.
The police talked to Alex a lot.
They asked him why his classmates did what they did,
but he told them he didn't know.
They asked him if there was a plan,
but he said if there was, he never heard it.
They asked him if there was a TV show
where someone ran away like that,
but he said if there was, he never saw it.
They also talked to Mrs. Gandy a whole bunch,
but she also didn't know anything
they kept the school closed for their big investigation.
But after a while, they had to open everything back up,
so that the kids that didn't disappear could learn again.
they had a big meeting at the school.
There was a bunch of counselors
to help everybody figure out how to feel
and be sad together, I guess.
This is where the story really starts.
What's important is that we don't judge our grief.
We might experience emotions we don't like.
We need to make sure that we allow ourselves
Anger is a very healthy part of the grief cycle.
It can be especially powerful in instances of abandonment.
Now, there are often times that we...

I'm saying it wouldn't be abnormal...
that you may call what happened "abandonment,"
but I don't. I don't see it like that.
I see something that doesn't make any sense at all.
We're talking about 17 kids in one classroom.

So you'll forgive me if I'm not

She's here. I wanna know exactly
